what_it_does: This file is a channel-memory attachment record that registers an image asset (target image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-01) under the design-source channel with attachment_type images and a creation timestamp.
observations: The file consists entirely of YAML front matter with no body content.
It declares an attachment id att-ea28dc77, channel design-source, and attachment_type images.
The target field uses an image:// URI pointing to design-source/presentation-15p/p-01.
The added timestamp is a precise ISO 8601 value with microsecond resolution and a UTC offset.
standouts: The target URI embeds both a collection name (presentation-15p) and a page identifier (p-01).
The file is a minimal typed pointer rather than a content-bearing document.
The timestamp includes sub-second precision and an explicit timezone.
conventions: Uses YAML front matter for structured metadata.
Attachment identifiers follow an att- prefix pattern.
References external assets via a custom image:// URI scheme.
Records creation time in ISO 8601 format with timezone.
concerns: The image target cannot be verified from this file alone.
There is no caption, alt text, or description of the referenced image.
No author or provenance beyond the timestamp is recorded.
No checksum or version is provided for the referenced asset.
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that points to page 1 of a 15-page design-source presentation image.
questions: What does the image at design-source/presentation-15p/p-01 depict?
Who added this attachment and why?
Is p-01 the first page of a specific presentation deck?
Are there corresponding attachments for the other 14 pages?
intention_signals: It appears intended to catalog a specific presentation image as a design-source channel attachment so the image can be located and associated with channel memory.
novelty: The image:// URI encodes both a collection (presentation-15p) and a page identifier (p-01), making the attachment a typed slide-level pointer.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, typed link between a channel-memory attachment entry and an external image asset, preserving the identity and location of a design-source presentation page for later retrieval.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file that registers an image asset at image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-01 under the design-source channel with type images and an ISO timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to record and link a specific design-source presentation image page into the channel-memory ledger.
